item.title: Sensex and Nifty Advance as IT Sector Leads Gains; Realty Faces Headwinds
item.description: Indian equity benchmarks advanced modestly on 20 Mar 2026, with the Sensex rising 325.72 points (0.44%) to close at 74,532.96 and the Nifty 50 gaining 112.35 points (0.49%) to end at 23,114.50. The market witnessed broad-based sectoral participation, led by Information Technology, while Realty remained the only major laggard. Midcap stocks outperformed, signalling selective investor appetite amid mixed global cues. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Sees Moderate Gains Led by Steel and Energy Stocks
item.description: The large-cap segment of the market demonstrated modest gains on 20 Mar 2026, with the BSE 100 index rising by 0.49%. While cyclical heavyweights showed varied performances, defensive stocks such as ONGC and Bharat Electron attracted renewed investor interest, reflecting a cautious yet optimistic market mood. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Shows Resilient Gains Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: The small-cap segment, represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has demonstrated steady resilience with a modest gain of 0.47% on the day and a 0.28% increase over the past five trading sessions. This performance underscores the segment’s cautious optimism amid broader market fluctuations, with notable divergences in sectoral trends and stock-specific momentum shaping the overall landscape. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Shows Resilient Gains Led by Select Stocks
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, demonstrated steady resilience on 20 Mar 2026, advancing 0.66% amid mixed sectoral performances and a positive breadth ratio. This marks a continuation of the segment’s recent upward momentum, with a 0.43% gain recorded over the past five trading sessions, underscoring investor confidence in mid-sized companies despite broader market uncertainties. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: JSW Holdings Ltd Hits Intraday Low Amid Price Pressure on 20 Mar 2026
item.description: JSW Holdings Ltd experienced significant intraday weakness on 20 Mar 2026, hitting a new 52-week low of Rs. 14,918 as the stock faced intense price pressure and heightened volatility, underperforming its sector and broader market indices. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Covance Softsol Leads Micro Cap Stocks with Exceptional 324.85% Half-Year Return
item.description: Covance Softsol has delivered an extraordinary return of 324.85% over the past six months, outpacing its micro cap peers and the broader market benchmarks by a significant margin. This remarkable performance highlights the stock’s strong fundamentals, attractive valuation, and positive technical momentum, positioning it as a standout performer in the Computers - Software & Consulting sector. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Covance Softsol Leads Market Rally with Exceptional 4197% Return in One Year
item.description: Covance Softsol has delivered an extraordinary return of 4197.4% over the past year, vastly outperforming its micro cap peers and broader market benchmarks. This remarkable surge highlights the stock’s strong fundamentals, technical momentum, and attractive valuation, positioning it as a standout performer in the Computers - Software & Consulting sector. Time: 2 hours ago

item.title: Petronet LNG Ltd. Hits Intraday Low Amid Price Pressure on 20 Mar 2026
item.description: Petronet LNG Ltd. experienced a notable decline today, touching an intraday low of Rs 257.3, marking a 5.2% drop as the stock faced significant price pressure amid broader sectoral and market challenges. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: LTIMindtree Ltd Sees Significant Open Interest Surge Amid Bearish Price Action
item.description: LTIMindtree Ltd (LTM), a large-cap player in the Computers - Software & Consulting sector, has witnessed a notable 12.75% increase in open interest in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity despite the stock’s recent underperformance. This surge in open interest, coupled with declining price trends and subdued investor participation, suggests evolving market positioning and potential directional bets among traders. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Shriram Finance Sees Notable Surge in Derivatives Open Interest Amid Mixed Price Action
item.description: Shriram Finance Ltd (SHRIRAMFIN) has witnessed a notable 11.04% increase in open interest (OI) in its derivatives segment, signalling heightened market activity and shifting investor positioning. Despite a recent dip in share price and a downgrade in its Mojo Grade from Buy to Hold, the surge in OI alongside rising volumes suggests a complex interplay of directional bets and hedging strategies among traders. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: National Aluminium Company Ltd Sees High-Value Trading Amid Consecutive Declines
item.description: National Aluminium Company Ltd (NATIONALUM), a mid-cap player in the Non-Ferrous Metals sector, witnessed significant trading activity on 20 March 2026, with over 2 crore shares exchanging hands and a total traded value exceeding ₹7,395 crores. Despite this high liquidity and institutional interest, the stock has experienced a notable downward trend, falling 3.88% on the day and continuing a three-day losing streak that has eroded 8.33% of its value. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: ITC Ltd. Sees Exceptional Volume Surge Amid Mixed Technical Signals
item.description: ITC Ltd., a heavyweight in the FMCG sector, witnessed one of the highest trading volumes on 20 March 2026, with over 2.69 crore shares exchanging hands. Despite a modest 1.96% gain on the day, the stock’s volume surge and technical indicators suggest a complex interplay of accumulation and distribution, warranting close attention from investors and market analysts alike. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Mid-Cap Segment Shows Steady Gains with Sectoral Divergence on 20 Mar 2026
item.description: The mid-cap segment, represented by the BSE MIDCAP 150 index, demonstrated steady resilience on 20 Mar 2026, closing with a gain of 0.57%. This performance underscores the segment’s relative strength amid mixed market conditions, supported by broad-based sectoral contributions and a positive advance-decline ratio. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Petronet LNG Sees Sharp Open Interest Surge Amid Price Weakness
item.description: Petronet LNG Ltd., a mid-cap player in the gas sector, has witnessed a notable surge in open interest in its derivatives segment, even as the stock price continues to languish near a 52-week low. This divergence between rising market participation and declining price levels signals a complex positioning landscape that investors and traders should carefully analyse. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Sensex Edges Higher as Banking Sector Leads Amid Mixed Mid and Small Cap Performance
item.description: Indian equity markets closed with modest gains on 20 Mar 2026, as the Sensex advanced 230.82 points or 0.31% to settle at 74,438.06. The Nifty mirrored this positive momentum, supported by broad-based sectoral strength, particularly in the banking space. However, mid and small cap indices showed a more nuanced picture with selective sectoral performances and notable volatility among individual stocks. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Large-Cap Segment Sees Mixed Momentum as JSW Steel Leads Gains and Hindalco Lags
item.description: The large-cap segment, represented by the BSE 100 index, recorded a modest gain of 0.37% on 20 Mar 2026, reflecting a cautious but positive market mood. While heavyweight stocks such as JSW Steel led the advance with a 3.20% return, others like Hindalco Industries lagged, posting a decline of 2.75%. The advance-decline ratio of 2.03x, with 67 stocks advancing against 33 declining, underscores a broadly constructive environment amid mixed sectoral trends. Time: 3 hours ago

item.title: Small-Cap Segment Shows Resilience with Moderate Gains and Positive Breadth
item.description: The small-cap segment, as represented by the BSE SMALLCAP 250 index, has demonstrated steady resilience with a modest gain of 0.42% on the day and a 0.23% rise over the past five sessions. Despite a mixed breadth and some technical call adjustments, the segment remains the best performer across market capitalisation categories, driven by notable stock-specific returns and sectoral dynamics. Time: 3 hours ago
